# Test for testcallgraph-coverage tool

# Create test file
mkdir -p test
exec cat > test/example.txt << 'EOF'
exec go test -cover ./...
exec mcpdiff --help
EOF

# Create mock coverage data
mkdir -p coverage/baseline
mkdir -p coverage/current

# Mock covdata output for baseline
exec cat > coverage/baseline/covdata.txt << 'EOF'
github.com/example/pkg1	75.0%	(15/20)
github.com/example/pkg2	60.0%	(12/20)
EOF

# Mock covdata output for current (with improvements)
exec cat > coverage/current/covdata.txt << 'EOF'
github.com/example/pkg1	80.0%	(16/20)
github.com/example/pkg2	65.0%	(13/20)
github.com/example/pkg3	50.0%	(10/20)
EOF

# Test basic coverage analysis
exec testcallgraph-coverage -test test/example.txt -coverage coverage/current
stdout 'Programs executed:'
stdout 'go.*test.*mcpdiff'
stdout 'Total coverage:'

# Test differential coverage
exec testcallgraph-coverage -test test/example.txt -coverage coverage/current -diff -baseline coverage/baseline
stdout 'Differential coverage:'
stdout 'Baseline:'
stdout 'Current:'
stdout 'Increase:'
stdout 'New packages:.*pkg3'
stdout 'Improved packages:'
stdout 'pkg1.*+5.0%'
stdout 'pkg2.*+5.0%'

# Test threshold filtering
exec testcallgraph-coverage -test test/example.txt -coverage coverage/current -diff -baseline coverage/baseline -threshold 10.0
! stdout 'example.txt'  # Should filter out because increase is less than 10%

# Test JSON output
exec testcallgraph-coverage -test test/example.txt -coverage coverage/current -format json
stdout '"test_file":'
stdout '"programs":'
stdout '"coverage":'
stdout '"total_coverage":'

# Test error handling
exec testcallgraph-coverage -test nonexistent.txt -coverage coverage/current
stderr 'Error'

# Test help
exec testcallgraph-coverage
stdout 'Usage:'
stdout 'Options:'
stdout 'Examples:'
